match_result.reasons:
Rudra is a recent graduate (graduating September 2025) with strong foundational ML/AI technical skills highly relevant to modern AI applications. He demonstrates excellent proficiency in Python, core ML frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit-learn), and contemporary AI tools (LangChain, RAG, LLMs). His technical skillset is solid for an entry-level AI position.

However, there's a significant domain mismatch. The Deloitte Audit & Assurance AI Engineer role requires specialized knowledge of audit methodologies, compliance frameworks, financial controls, and risk assessment—none of which appear in the candidate's profile. Audit is a distinct domain with its own practices, tools, and regulatory requirements (e.g., PCAOB, GAAS, SOX compliance). The role likely requires understanding how AI applies to audit-specific challenges like testing controls, detecting anomalies in financial data, and ensuring audit quality.

The candidate's background focuses on GenAI, NLP, RAG, and LLMs—areas that may have limited direct application in traditional audit workflows. While AI can be applied to audit (anomaly detection, data quality assessment), the audit & assurance context requires domain knowledge the candidate hasn't demonstrated.

Deloitte typically expects even junior candidates to have some awareness of their service lines or be willing to develop domain expertise. This is more of a domain transition role than a perfect junior match. The candidate would need significant onboarding and domain training, which is possible but not ideal for a graduate hire.
